title: Build external call calldata directly in scratch/free memory without abi.encode
description: Instead of using abi.encodeWithSelector which allocates new memory and encodes arguments with padding, this pattern writes the 4-byte function selector and arguments directly into known memory positions. The selector is written with alignment to 0x1c so that call(gas(), target, 0, 0x1c, 0x64, ...) sends exactly the right calldata bytes. Saves memory allocation, free pointer update, and ABI encoding gas.
pattern_after:
mstore(0x60, amount)
mstore(0x40, to)
mstore(0x2c, shl(96, from))
mstore(0x0c, 0x23b872dd000000000000000000000000) // transferFrom selector
call(gas(), token, 0, 0x1c, 0x64, 0x00, 0x20)
when_to_apply: Any external call where the function signature and argument types are known at compile time
when_not_to_apply: Dynamic-length arguments (bytes, string, arrays) that require ABI offset encoding
